pi_items.source: Peer-reviewed literature: normal adult ICV ranges 1100-1660 mL (mean 1289-1469 mL); 800 mL is below healthy adult range and represents severe microcephaly (Pubmed 8847593, 33111974, PMC10517302)
Peer-reviewed literature: normal adult ICV upper limit ~1571 mL for men (1469+102 mL) and ~1400 mL for women; 1900 mL exceeds healthy adult range by 300+ mL (Pubmed 8847593, PMC10517302)

Peer-reviewed literature: lower boundary of normal adult ICV range is 1106-1178 mL based on mean ± SD calculations (Pubmed 8847593: men 1469±102 mL, women 1289±111 mL; PMC10517302: range 1106-1656 mL)
Peer-reviewed literature: upper boundary of normal adult ICV range is ~1571 mL for men and ~1400 mL for women; 1700 mL exceeds the upper normal limit by 100-300 mL (Pubmed 8847593, PMC10517302)

Environment setup script at benchmarks/cua_world/environments/invesalius3_env/scripts/setup_invesalius.sh downloads from https://github.com/invesalius/invesalius3/releases/download/v3.0/0051.zip; InVesalius is open-source medical imaging software (GitHub invesalius/invesalius3)

privileged_info_summary: The task uses a real CT cranium dataset (case 0051) from InVesalius's official v3.0 sample data, not synthetic data. The dataset represents a normal adult human skull scan. The metadata volume constraints are partially incorrect: the claimed minimum (800 mL) is below the healthy adult ICV range (1100-1660 mL) and represents severe microcephaly; the claimed maximum (1900 mL) significantly exceeds the typical adult upper limit (~1571 mL); the optimal minimum (1100 mL) correctly represents the lower boundary of normal adult ICV; the optimal maximum (1700 mL) exceeds reported upper limits by 100-300 mL. Actual peer-reviewed data shows normal adult intracranial volumes average 1469 ± 102 mL in men and 1289 ± 111 mL in women.
